mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC signaling kinases to reduce AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3+ AML; text notes 'top 1–3 µM' and range given as 0.1 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3/RAF/VEGFR-family kinases to suppress growth and signaling
additional_notes: Used off-label in FLT3-ITD AML; range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases including FLT3 activity
additional_notes: Range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Selective inhibition of FLT3 signaling
additional_notes: Clinical/approved in some regions; text notes 'top 100–300 nM'

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 to block oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; text notes 'top 300 nM–1 µM' and range given as 0.1 nM–1 µM

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases impacting downstream signaling dependencies
additional_notes: Range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to suppress MAPK pathway signaling
additional_notes: Text notes activity in subsets of AML; 'top 100–300 nM'

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin/transcription and induce growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Alternative name SAHA (removed from primary drug_name); approved for CTCL; range given as 10 nM–30 µM

mechanism: Inhibits HDACs to promote transcriptional reprogramming and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; text notes 'top 100–300 nM'

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to modulate gene expression and reduce proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for PTCL; range given as 10 nM–30 µM

mechanism: At high concentrations exerts HDAC-inhibitory effects altering transcription
additional_notes: Text notes 'HDAC effects at high µM–mM' and widely tested in AML; 'top 2–5 mM'

mechanism: Incorporates into nucleic acids and inhibits DNA methylation to promote hypomethylation and differentiation
additional_notes: Approved for AML/MDS; range given as 1 nM–10 µM; text suggests top 3–10 µM and schedule dependence

mechanism: Nucleoside analog that inhibits DNA methyltransferase leading to DNA hypomethylation
additional_notes: Approved for AML; range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM; text suggests top 1–3 µM

mechanism: Selectively inhibits BCL-2 to trigger mitochondrial apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for AML combinations; described as often very potent; range given as 0.01 nM–1 µM; text notes 'top 100 nM–1 µM'

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-XL to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Alternative name ABT-263 (removed from primary drug_name); clinical; range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome causing accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma; range given as 0.03 nM–300 nM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome leading to proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma; range given as 0.03 nM–300 nM

category: Apoptosis/stress pathway drug (arsenic compound; APL therapy)
mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and apoptosis; clinically targets PML-RARA in APL
additional_notes: Approved for APL; broader AML effects reported; range given as 10 nM–10 µM; text notes 'top 3–10 µM'

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I causing energetic/metabolic stress
additional_notes: Range given as 0.1 mM–50 mM; text notes strong dependence on media glucose and 'top 10–50 mM'

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase to disrupt redox homeostasis and induce oxidative stress
additional_notes: Historically approved for rheumatoid arthritis; range given as 1 nM–3 µM; text notes 'top 1–3 µM'
